2605.27110.title: BAIT: Boundary-Guided Disclosure Escalation via Self-Conditioned Reasoning
2605.27110.paper_summary: In this work, we propose BAIT (Boundary-Aware Iterative Trap), a three-step jailbreak framework that approaches malicious goals through internal disclosure. BAIT first asks the model to identify the protection boundary, then requires it to refine that boundary, and finally requests a detailed example. By expanding each step upon the model's previous responses, BAIT turns the model's own reasoning and consistency tendency into a disclosure pathway. Experiments on AdvBench, JailbreakBench, AIR-Bench, and SORRY-Bench demonstrate that BAIT consistently achieves strong attack success rates across top-tier large language models, significantly advancing conventional jailbreak baselines. Further analysis reveals that: 1) prevention-oriented framing significantly outperforms direct knowledge request; 2) the refinement step plays a critical role in disclosure escalation; and 3) the first two steps have a certain chance of eliciting harmful content while triggering little filtering.
2605.27110.paper_summary_zh: 在這項工作中，我們提出了 BAIT（邊界感知迭代陷阱），這是一個三步驟的越獄框架，通過內部披露來接近惡意目標。<lb/>BAIT 首先要求模型識別保護邊界，然後要求它細化該邊界，最後請求一個詳細的例子。<lb/>通過擴展每一步基於模型之前的回應，BAIT 將模型自身的推理和一致性傾向轉化為披露途徑。<lb/>在 AdvBench、JailbreakBench、AIR-Bench 和 SORRY-Bench 上的實驗表明，BAIT 在頂級大型語言模型中始終實現強大的攻擊成功率，顯著推進了傳統越獄基準。<lb/>進一步分析顯示：1）以預防為導向的框架顯著優於直接的知識請求；2）細化步驟在披露升級中起著關鍵作用；3）前兩個步驟在引發有害內容的同時觸發的過濾很少。
2605.27110.author: Xuan Luo et.al.
2605.27110.authors: Xuan Luo, Yue Wang, Geng Tu, Jing Li, Ruifeng Xu
